code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about a written work into a single fluent sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Helper to produce a phrase for each predicate
    def phrase(triple: Triple, is_main: bool) -> str:
        s, p, o = triple.subject, triple.predicate, triple.object
        # Basic patterns for common predicates
        patterns = {
            "author": f"{s} was written by {o}",
            "isbnNumber": f"ISBN {o}",
            "followedBy": f"followed by {o}",
            "precededBy": f"preceded by {o}",
            "language": f"written in {o}",
            "mediaType": f"available as {o}",
            "numberOfPages": f"{o} pages",
            "country": f"originates from {o}",
            "almaMater": f"the author studied at {o}",
            "doctoralAdvisor": f"the author was advised by {o}",
            "nationality": f"the author is {o}",
            "residence": f"the author lives in {o}",
            "codenCode": f"CODEN {o}",
            "issnNumber": f"ISSN {o}",
            "LCCN_number": f"LCCN {o}",
            "abbreviation": f"abbreviated as {o}",
            "academicDiscipline": f"belongs to the discipline of {o}",
            "publisher": f"published by {o}",
            "firstPublicationYear": f"first published in {o}",
            "editor": f"edited by {o}",
            "impactFactor": f"has an impact factor of {o}",
            "oclcNumber": f"OCLC number {o}",
            "libraryofCongressClassification": f"classified under {o}",
            "genre": f"a {o} work",
            "literaryGenre": f"a {o} work",
            "eissnNumber": f"e‑ISSN {o}",
            "frequency": f"issued {o}",
            "headquarter": f"headquartered in {o}",
            "leader": f"leader is {o}",
            "birthPlace": f"born in {o}",
            "affiliation": f"affiliated with {o}",
            "city": f"located in {o}",
            "president": f"president is {o}",
            "state": f"in the state of {o}",
            "spokenIn": f"spoken in {o}",
            "influencedBy": f"influenced by {o}",
            "family": f"belongs to the {o} family",
            "ethnicGroup": f"associated with the {o} ethnic group",
            "largestCity": f"largest city is {o}",
            "location": f"located in {o}",
            "deathPlace": f"died in {o}",
            "notableWork": f"notable work includes {o}",
            "parentCompany": f"owned by {o}",
            "regionServed": f"serves the region of {o}",
            "founder": f"founded by {o}",
            "capital": f"capital is {o}",
            "demonym": f"demonym is {o}",
            "leaderTitle": f"leader holds the title of {o}",
            "nickname": f"nicknamed {o}",
            "genus": f"belongs to the genus {o}",
            "releaseDate": f"released on {o}",
            "birthDate": f"born on {o}",
        }
        # Default fallback keeps the original triple wording
        default = f"{s} {p} {o}"
        phrase_text = patterns.get(p, default)

        # Ensure the subject appears explicitly unless already present
        if not phrase_text.startswith(s):
            return f"{s} {phrase_text}"
        return phrase_text

    # Determine the primary subject (the subject of the first triple)
    main_subject = triples[0].subject

    # Build phrases for the main subject first, then for any secondary subjects
    main_phrases = [phrase(t, True) for t in triples if t.subject == main_subject]
    other_phrases = [phrase(t, False) for t in triples if t.subject != main_subject]

    all_phrases = main_phrases + other_phrases

    # Combine the phrases into a fluent sentence
    if len(all_phrases) == 1:
        sentence = all_phrases[0] + "."
    else:
        sentence = ", ".join(all_phrases[:-1]) + ", and " + all_phrases[-1] + "."

    # Capitalize the first character of the final sentence
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

metadata.changes: Change 1: '"country": f"published in {o}",' to '"country": f"originates from {o}",'

You are an expert data engineer specializing in converting data to text. You will be given the current Python program with its BLUE score and an inspiration program and your task will be to evolve the current Python program to improve the score. It is a program that converts a list of triples into natural language text. The given triples will be associated with the topic: WrittenWork. The program must implement a function called 'predict' that accepts a list of triples and generates a coherent, contextually relevant sentence that accurately represents the information contained in the triples. Ensure that the generated text is fluent, grammatically correct, and maintains the meaning of the original data. There can be multiple triples given that make up a complex sentence. Converting all those triples into one sentence will award greater score. An example of a complex sentence:
Triples:
(Antwerp | cityServed | Antwerp International Airport)
(Belgium | country | Antwerp)
(City of Brussels | capital | Belgium)
Example sentence:
"Antwerp International Airport serves the city of Antwerp which is in Belgium, where the capital is Brussels."

The 'predict' function returns that sentence as a string. Below is the list of all possible predicates that can be given as an input. With the given predicates and example triples implement the 'predict' function so it can process all the possible predicates:

Predicate: author - Example triple: (1634: The Baltic War | author | David Weber)
